title: asclepias macrotis
intro: Asclepias macrotis, also known as the large-flowered milkweed or butterfly milkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the milkweed family (Apocynaceae). It is a vital food source for monarch butterflies and other pollinators, as its nectar-rich flowers provide essential nutrients. The plant's roots, stems, and leaves possess medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Asclepias macrotis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been traditionally used to promote wound healing, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function.
